hero.subtitle: The HTML-first framework for building fast, interactive web applications.

features.title: Engineered for Simplicity
features.subtitle: NoJS replaces modern JavaScript framework overhead with native HTML directives that manage state, fetching, and routing.
features.f1Desc: Write reactive logic directly in your markup using attributes like state, bind, and foreach. No Virtual DOM or compile step needed.
features.f2Desc: Perform GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE calls directly via attributes. Manage loading states, skeletons, and errors dynamically.
features.f3Desc: Full client-side router supporting path params, query parameters, route guards, nested layouts, and fluid native View Transitions.
features.f4Desc: A single standalone script with zero dependencies. Includes state management, data fetching, SPA routing, i18n translation, and animations.

directives.title: Clean, Declarative Directives
directives.subtitle: Express application logic right in the elements. Lexically scoped reactive contexts propagate down the DOM.
directives.s1Desc: Define reactive scopes using state="...". Bind fields in two-way synchronicity using model for inputs, textareas, and select menus.
directives.s2Desc: Toggle parts of the DOM using if/else-if/else (which adds/removes elements entirely) or show/hide (which toggles CSS display properties).
directives.s3Desc: Render lists seamlessly with foreach="item in list". Auto-provides helper variables such as $index, $first, $last, $even, and $odd.

http.title: Zero-JS Data Fetching
http.subtitle: Query APIs, cache responses, and handle loaders natively through declarative properties.
http.featureDesc: Placeholders automatically hide when data arrives. Any URL segment or query parameter enclosing {variable} automatically triggers a new fetch whenever that state updates.
http.item1Desc: Pre-rendered skeleton="..." nodes occupy exact layout space before loading is done.
http.item2Desc: Add cached to store responses in memory, localStorage, or sessionStorage.
http.item3Desc: Add refresh="30000" to keep server data synchronized every 30 seconds.

getStarted.title: Getting Started
getStarted.subtitle: Two steps. No build tools, no package manager, no configuration.
getStarted.step1Desc: Add the NoJS Skill to your AI coding agent. It handles scaffolding, CDN setup, component generation, and expert guidance on every directive.
getStarted.step2Desc: Or drop the script tag directly into your HTML. NoJS auto-initializes on DOM ready and starts parsing directives immediately.

manifesto.col1p1: Somewhere between "component thinking" and "virtual DOM diffing", we forgot that the browser already understands HTML. It already knows how to update the DOM. It already handles events.
manifesto.col1p2: We built abstraction upon abstraction until a simple form required three files, a build pipeline, and a framework runtime.
manifesto.col2p1: No.JS is the opposite bet. The browser is not the enemy, it’s the platform. HTML is not limited, it’s underutilized. Attributes are not primitive, they’re a DSL waiting to happen.
manifesto.col2p2: We built No.JS on one conviction: if you can express it in HTML, you should.
manifesto.col3p1: The result is a framework you can learn in an afternoon and ship in 30 seconds. Not because we made it simple, but because the browser was already capable. We just got out of the way.
manifesto.col3p2: This is not anti-JavaScript. It’s pro-HTML.
